On the occasion of National Teacher's Day, Iran's President Pezeshkian says that scenes from the field of defending the homeland that have left the world astonished are all owed to the teachers who have taught our children lessons in faith, sacrifice, humanity, and love for Iran. https://t.co/gIaqnTbVSd

The Iranian mission to the United Nations issued a sharply worded statement on social media on Saturday, accusing the United States of failing to comply with its obligations under the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT).

In the post, the mission said the US record was “extremely SHAMEFUL,” arguing that Washington, “possessor of thousands of nuclear warheads and the No. 1 proliferator of such weapons,” has been in “clear non-compliance” with Articles I and VI of the NPT for 56 years.

It added that the United States “should not be given any cover for its outrageous and hypocritical behavior,” framing Washington’s nuclear policy as inconsistent with its international commitments on disarmament and non-proliferation.

The statement also defended Iran’s nuclear activities, asserting that there is “no restriction on the level of uranium enrichment” so long as it is carried out under the supervision of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), asserting that this was the case with Iran’s previous nuclear practices.

All ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz must pay a fee, as it is not international waters and owners have no choice. If the Trump regime forces them not to pay, ships will not be allowed to pass through, worsening the global economic crisis.

Iran marked Teacher’s Day by commemorating 26 educators killed in a US–Israeli attack on the Shajareh Tayyebeh Primary School in Minab, southern Iran.

The ceremony honored teachers who were among the victims of the aggression that targeted the school full of children and educational staff. 

The US-Israeli act of aggression, likely the most devastating incident throughout the aggression, killed more than 160 children 

Overall, 3,375 people have been killed in the aggression that spanned 40 days. Official figures indicate that 2,875 of those martyred were men and 496 were women, with four individuals remaining unidentified.

Hundreds of children were among the dead, including 255 aged between one and twelve years.

Président @drpezeshkian à l’occasion de la journée des enseignants en Iran:

Si, sur le terrain de la défense du pays, nous assistons à des scènes qui émerveillent le monde, c’est grâce aux enseignants qui ont inculqué à nos enfants la foi, le sacrifice, l’humanité et l’amour de l’Iran.

L’avenir de l’Iran se construit avec la solidarité, la bienveillance et le pardon. L’espoir de l’Iran, ce sont les enfants que vous éduquez.

Bonne fête à vous.

مینابا، مینابای من

دختران هفت دریا
چرا به سیاره گل‌ سرخ آمدید
این‌جا شیطان در تاریکی 

سکوت و سرنوشت شما را 
تصرف کرده است.
شیطانِ شبْ‌پرست خبر ندارد 
با نخستین تیغ آفتاب

شما با تن هزار پاره خود
از خلیج ‌فارس برمی‌خیزید
و خواب را در چشم نهنگِ کور
حرام خواهید کرد. 
«سیدعلی صالحی»

معلم معمار آینده و نگهبان آگاهی یک ملت است. در روز معلم، سالروز شهادت استاد مرتضی مطهری، به احترام همه آنان که چراغ دانایی را روشن نگه داشتند، سر تعظیم فرود می‌آوریم، به‌ویژه ۲۶ #معلم شهید مدرسه #شجره_طیبه #میناب که شمع آگاهی در دستشان به شعله بیداری یک ملت تبدیل شد و تاریکی را رسوا کرد.

In an interview for Al Mayadeen, Professor Marandi said he believes the chances of "conflict are very high,” noting that the United States did not deploy additional troops to the region after the 39-day Ramadan war merely to “sit there and do nothing.”

Addressing the messages exchanged between Iran and the United States, Marandi pointed out that such communication is not new, as it has been ongoing for decades, but has intensified in recent months and weeks due to the war.

He added that while Iran may present new proposals, it will not allow its sovereignty to be undermined. Professor Marandi explained that unless the United States comes to terms with the reality on several issues, including Iran’s peaceful nuclear program and its control of the Strait of Hormuz, "siege warfare" will continue, and "military conflict" is likely in the coming days or weeks.

@s_m_marandi 

#AlMayadeen #StraitofHormuz #IranWar #Trump
